skill.name: data-orchestrator
skill.description: Coordinates ETL, analytics, feature engineering, data quality, and pipeline governance tasks for AI-assisted data work.
skill.summary: Plan and coordinate data pipeline implementation with quality gates, lineage, and monitoring.
skill.author: Brownbull

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found these are Markdown examples, inline skill names, file references, and ordinary data-pipeline terminology with no executable command, cryptographic API, network call, or prompt injection evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Examples
low_findings.description: Static external command detections map to Markdown fenced blocks, inline skill names, and illustrative Python snippets. No shell execution instruction or command invocation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are documentation blocks or inline names only. They contain no executable Ruby, shell backtick execution, subprocess call, or user-controlled command path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections do not correspond to hashing, encryption, signing, or password handling. The referenced text describes skill metadata and an ETL step.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No cryptographic algorithm or security-sensitive data handling appears at the cited lines. The matches appear caused by nearby text patterns rather than actual crypto usage.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Language
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance detections are ordinary checklist and heading text for data testing and anti-patterns. They do not request host inventory, environment discovery, or privilege inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is data quality guidance, not system probing. No command, host path, credential source, or enumeration target is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Language
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance detections refer to monitoring pipeline progress and avoiding data loss. They do not describe port scanning, endpoint discovery, packet capture, or external requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The terms are used in a data pipeline operations context. There is no network API, hostname, scanning tool, or instruction to inspect network infrastructure.

content.value_statement: Data teams need consistent planning for pipelines, quality gates, and feature work. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured data orchestration checklist.
actual_capabilities: Structures ETL and ELT work into receive, prepare, assign, monitor, and validate steps.
Defines quality checks for completeness, accuracy, consistency, timeliness, uniqueness, and validity.
Coordinates feature engineering with dependency tracking, version control, edge cases, and monitoring.
Outlines integration points with backend, frontend, and documentation workflows.
Provides event examples for pipeline status and data source updates.
Promotes tests for transformations, pipeline flow, data quality, performance, edge cases, and regression.
limitations: It is a guidance-only skill and does not execute pipelines by itself.
It does not include runnable ETL scripts, connectors, or deployment automation.
It assumes other data skills or project tooling perform implementation work.
It cannot verify data quality unless connected to actual project tests and metrics.

use_cases.title: Plan a New ETL Pipeline
use_cases.description: Break a new ingestion and transformation request into sources, dependencies, quality checks, tests, and delivery outputs.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Feature Engineering
use_cases.description: Define feature logic, upstream dependencies, monitoring needs, and quality expectations before implementation.

use_cases.title: Review Analytics Readiness
use_cases.description: Check whether a reporting workflow has freshness rules, lineage, validation, documentation, and dashboard contracts.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Pipeline Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for structured pipeline planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the data-orchestrator skill to plan a pipeline for my data source. Include dependencies, quality checks, tests, and expected outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Define Quality Gates
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the data-orchestrator skill to define quality gates for this dataset. Cover completeness, accuracy, consistency, timeliness, uniqueness, and validity.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate Feature Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the data-orchestrator skill to coordinate a feature engineering task. Identify feature logic, upstream data, edge cases, monitoring, and test requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Pipeline Readiness
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced readiness review across implementation, testing, and operations.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the data-orchestrator skill to audit this pipeline plan. Find missing validation, recovery, documentation, lineage, monitoring, and integration requirements.

output_examples.input: Plan a daily revenue aggregation pipeline.
output_examples.output: A structured plan with source checks, transformation steps, quality gates, checkpointing, monitoring, documentation updates, and success metrics.

output_examples.input: Review a churn risk feature before implementation.
output_examples.output: A readiness review covering dependencies, business logic, edge cases, performance tests, feature store updates, and production monitoring.

output_examples.input: Check whether an analytics dashboard has reliable data inputs.
output_examples.output: A validation checklist for freshness, API contracts, lineage, completeness, metric definitions, and regression coverage.

best_practices: Provide source tables, expected outputs, freshness needs, and downstream consumers before asking for orchestration.
Ask for explicit quality gates and test requirements for every pipeline or feature request.
Pair the skill with project-specific standards, schemas, and monitoring tools for implementation.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a substitute for real data validation or production monitoring.
Do not skip lineage and documentation when changing pipeline behavior.
Do not ask it to implement connectors without providing project tooling and access constraints.

faq.question: Does this skill run ETL jobs?
faq.answer: No. It provides orchestration guidance, checklists, and planning structure for AI-assisted data work.

faq.question: Can it help with data quality?
faq.answer: Yes. It emphasizes completeness, accuracy, consistency, timeliness, uniqueness, validity, and test coverage.

faq.question: Is it useful for feature engineering?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers feature logic, dependencies, versioning, edge cases, performance tests, and monitoring.

faq.question: Does it include connectors or scripts?
faq.answer: No. The skill contains guidance and examples, not runnable data connectors or deployment scripts.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide data sources, target outputs, SLAs, quality rules, dependencies, and downstream consumers.
